entitySuffixRule: Must contain the words "Limited Liability Company" or the abbreviation "L.L.C." or "LLC."
distinguishabilityNote: Must be distinguishable upon the records of the Secretary of State from every entity name, reserved name, and registered name already on file. Entity-type designators (LLC, Inc., Corp., and the like) are disregarded in the comparison, so a different ending alone does not make a name distinguishable. Alabama is one of the few states where a name reservation is mandatory: a domestic LLC must obtain a Certificate of Name Reservation before it can file its Certificate of Formation, and the reserved name must match the name on the formation document.
nameReservationCostBasis: The Secretary of State fee is $25 by paper and $28 online (the online path carries the small electronic-access surcharge). Because the reservation is a required step before formation, this is a mandatory cost for every domestic Alabama LLC, not an optional hold.
notes: Alabama's defining rule is the mandatory name reservation: unlike almost every other state, you cannot file LLC formation documents until the Secretary of State has issued a Certificate of Name Reservation for that exact name (§ 10A-1-4.02(f)). The reservation is effective for one year or until the applicant withdraws it (§ 10A-1-5.14) and can be renewed. If an LLC name includes "bank," "trust," "insurance," or "engineer," the applicant must attach a letter of approval from the governing agency; the flat bans on "corporation"/"incorporated" apply to limited partnerships rather than LLCs. Alabama has no LLC annual report (it collects a separate Business Privilege Tax instead).
